t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Card network identifier. Active brands: 1=Visa, 2=MasterCard, 3=Diners, 8=Maestro. Inactive: 0=None, 4=Amex, 5=FirePay, 6=JCB, 7=American Express, 9=Laser, 10=Switch, 11=UK Local, 12=Discover, 13=Local Card, 14=China UnionPay, 15=Solo, 16=Cirrus, 17=GE Capital, 18=Unknown, 19-31=various regional/legacy brands.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Card brand name. Unique constraint prevents duplicates. Used in payment UI, transaction records, and fraud reporting.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Card brand name. Unique constraint prevents duplicates in production. Used in payment UI, transaction records, and fraud reporting. Renamed from `Name` in production. 0=None, 1=Visa, 2=Master Card, 3=Diners, 4=Amex, 5=Fire Pay, 6=JCB, 7=American Express, 8=Maestro, 9=Laser, 10=Switch, 11=UK Local Credit Card, 12=Discover, 13=Local Card, 14=China Union Pay, 15=Solo, 16=Cirrus, 17=GE Capital.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Added 'in production' qualifier, rename note, and full value list — core upstream text preserved with additions

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Whether this card brand is currently accepted for deposits: 1=accepted, 0=rejected. DEFAULT 1 (new card types are active by default). Only 4 of 32 are currently active.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Whether this card brand is currently accepted for deposits: 1=accepted, 0=rejected. Type widened from bit to int in DWH. Only 4 of 32 are currently active in production. DWH note: DWH snapshot values may differ from current production state.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped 'DEFAULT 1 (new card types are active by default)' — semantic loss of default value behavior. Replaced with DWH-specific notes not present in upstream.

issues.problem: Tier 1 upstream description dropped 'DEFAULT 1 (new card types are active by default)' and replaced with DWH-specific notes. This is a semantic loss — the default behavior for new card types is meaningful business context that was removed.
Truncated upstream description by removing '18=Unknown, 19-31=various regional/legacy brands'. While the DWH only has 18 rows, the Tier 1 instruction is to quote verbatim from upstream.
No explicit Phase Gate Checklist section with P2/P3 checkboxes. Footer lacks a phases-completed list, making it impossible to verify which data-gathering phases were run.
No NULL-rate or distribution analysis mentioned for any of the 4 columns, all of which are nullable in the DDL.
Added 'in production' qualifier and full value enumeration to upstream description. While these are additions rather than losses, they deviate from verbatim upstream quoting.

verdict.regeneration_feedback: Re-run with: (1) Restore IsActive description to verbatim upstream text: 'Whether this card brand is currently accepted for deposits: 1=accepted, 0=rejected. DEFAULT 1 (new card types are active by default). Only 4 of 32 are currently active.' — append DWH-specific notes (type widening, snapshot divergence) AFTER the verbatim text, clearly separated. (2) Restore CardTypeID tail: include '18=Unknown, 19-31=various regional/legacy brands' from upstream, with a DWH-scoping note appended. (3) Add Phase Gate Checklist section or integrate P2/P3 completion status into footer.

skipped_phases: P2/P3 checklist not present — cannot verify
